parameters.analytical_note: Ford fleet brochures and dealer marketing confirm F-450 models use a wide-track monobeam front axle. However, the exact design variant (e.g. Dana Super 60 non-driving vs driving variant housing modifications) remains unverified at the engineering document level.

A value of 1899.92 mm (74.8 inches) is a highly probable candidate based on previous documentation passes, but the precise page reference, measuring plane parameters, and tolerances are unverified without the primary PDF file.

Chassis cab standards historically target 34 inches nominal outside rail spacing, but the exact measurement boundaries (including web vs flange endpoints) must be visually confirmed on the engineering schematic.
F-450 10-lug wheel pattern historically uses a 225 mm PCD. Verifying this against the 2019 Order Guide or assembly drawings is pending primary document availability.
Chassis cab rail thicknesses are highly variable by GVWR rating. The claimed 7.1 mm value is flagged as a candidate needing layout sheet verification.
